brand.name: Clarusway
brand.tagline: Get job ready in 5 to 9 months! No IT background required.

offer.what: Online IT bootcamp programs covering Cloud & DevOps Engineering (AWS), Data Science & Applied AI, Software Development (Full Stack), Salesforce, Cybersecurity, Web3 & Blockchain, and SDET. Programs range from 3 to 8 months, part-time (16–20 hrs/week), fully online with live classes and LMS access.
offer.coreOutcome: Career change into tech: graduates land IT jobs averaging $90K–$102K starting salary with a reported 91–92% job placement rate.
offer.pricePoint: $7,000–$8,000 per program. Payment options: upfront (30% discount), monthly installments (~$250/mo), ISA (12.5% of income for 18 months post-hire, kicks in above $40K salary, capped at $20K), 0% interest education credit, and loans via Meritize.
offer.commitment: Part-time, 3–8 months depending on track; 16–20 hours/week; fully online; 15-day risk-free trial with full refund.
leadMagnets: CW Mini Bootcamp – Free (50+ hours of Python, Linux, Git, SQL)
2-minute Career Path Quiz (delivers personalized IT path + discount coupon)
Free Career Consultation / Talk to an Advisor

audience.icp: Career changers aged 25–45 with zero IT background — former delivery drivers, marketers, stay-at-home parents — who want a well-paying tech job but are overwhelmed by where to start and unsure they can afford or handle it.
painPoints: No tech background or programming experience — feel unqualified
Afraid of wasting money on something they might not finish
Unclear which IT path (DevOps vs. cyber vs. data) suits them
Balancing study with a full-time job or family responsibilities
Skeptical about bootcamp job placement claims
segments: Complete career changers (no IT background) — core audience
Working IT professionals looking to upskill or certify
Corporate / enterprise clients seeking custom team training

salesCycle.length: Days to 2–3 weeks (awareness → free resource/info session → advisor call → application → enrollment)
salesCycle.trial: Yes — 15-day risk-free trial with full refund. Also a free mini bootcamp (50+ hrs) as top-of-funnel trial and a free career consultation call.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Advisor-assisted: prospect books a free career consultation → discovery call → personalized program recommendation → application form → payment/ISA agreement. Self-serve application also available.
salesCycle.topObjection: "I have no IT background / I'm not technical enough" — closely followed by cost and time commitment concerns.

ahaMoment: Completing the first live hands-on project in the free mini bootcamp or early in the paid program — the moment a total beginner successfully runs code or configures a cloud service and realizes it is learnable. Career Management System orientation (resume, LinkedIn, mock interview) also triggers a high-value second aha for graduates.

retention.recurring: Non-recurring per enrollment; revenue is one-time per course. Alumni can enroll in additional certifications or advanced courses.
retention.expansion: Upsell to longer/combined tracks (e.g., AWS & DevOps 8-month flagship vs. DevOps-only 3-month); corporate custom training (enterprise arm); alumni referring new students.
retention.churnRisk: Mid-program dropout due to overwhelm, life events, or falling behind in live sessions. No-show on advisor consultation calls before enrollment is the top pre-purchase churn signal.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding: likely thin — new enrollees may not receive a structured email sequence driving them to the activation event (first class / LMS login)
Lead-Magnet Nurture: Mini Bootcamp and Quiz leads likely under-nurtured; need a conversion sequence turning free users into paid applicants
Trial/Consult Conversion: Prospects who book but haven't enrolled (post-advisor-call drop-off) likely lack a structured follow-up sequence
Booking & Reminder: No-show reduction for free consultation and info session bookings
Value Newsletter: Blog content exists but email newsletter engagement is unknown — likely inconsistent
Renewal / Expansion: Alumni progression to next certification or referral ask — almost certainly missing
Win-Back / Re-Engagement: Lapsed leads (took quiz, downloaded mini bootcamp, never applied) — likely no re-engagement automation
toneVoice: Motivational, accessible, and results-obsessed. Copy leans into transformation stories and outcome specificity ('$102K average salary', '92% job placement'). Warmly direct — not salesy or corporate. Speaks to the hesitant beginner with reassurance ('No IT background? No problem'). Uses plain language; avoids jargon unless teaching a concept. Community and mentor-forward.
compliance: CAN-SPAM compliant (US-based email); ISA agreements governed by applicable state lending/education finance law; standard ed-tech data handling. No HIPAA, no financial advice constraints. GDPR consideration for any EU learners.
